summary: Leymebamba highland large numerical register (176 cords, 1 STRING / 157 INT / 18 EMPTY = 89 percent numerical) at the Centro Mallqui (Laguna de los Condores, CMA 480/LC1-109.1). Architecture 1-PAIRE relational L2. Dominant color W (white) at 45 percent (80 cords), plus AB 45, GG 18, KB 15, MB 11. EXTREME SEGMENTATION: 30 clusters in 176 cords = avg 5.9 cords / cluster. Alternating pattern: 22 MULTI-CORD DETAIL clusters (6-10 cords each, carrying per-unit measurements ranging 1-71) interleaved with 6 SINGLE-CORD MILESTONE clusters on W (mostly) carrying ISOLATED large values (112, 106, 125, 153, 92) plus one closing AB 10. The single STRING cord at position 41 reads paqa (dawn / east) on AB color with knot sequence S10 L6 E = numerical prefix 10 followed by pa+qa syllables = DIRECTIONAL ANCHOR. 68 Ascher pendant-pendant sums declared but 0/48 verified -- LARGE declarative Ascher structure that does not close, unusual anomaly. Cascade depth 4 on the (unverified) sum graph. Total numerical value = 2913. CLASSIFICATION: numerical_record, promoting UR087 from singleton to N=2 and extending the genre to highland Chachapoyas. Alternative reading: cadastral_survey with detail-block + milestone structure. PROMOTES numerical_record from singleton to N=2 with the FIRST HIGHLAND instance. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onset is L6 pa, not L10 / L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  176 cords. Architecture 1-PAIRE relational (depth L2 with subsidiaries; depth 4 in Ascher graph).
  30 clusters of varying sizes from 1 to 10 cords. Pattern: mostly multi-cord detail clusters interleaved with occasional single-cord milestone clusters.
  Composition: INT 157 (89 percent), STRING 1, EMPTY 18.
  Provenance: Leymebamba, Amazonas, Peru. Museum: Centro Mallqui (CMA 480/LC1-109.1). SAME sub-collection (CMA 480) as UR007 (CMA 480/LC1-109.4).
  Colors: W (white) 80 DOMINANT, AB 45, GG 18, KB 15, MB 11, plus minor LK and RL.
  KFG twin KH0246.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  1/1 STRING cord decoded (paqa, 100 percent dict hit). The single STRING carries a NUMERICAL PREFIX (S10) alongside its syllabic content -- dual reading encoding.
  157 INT values clean, no L?.

ASCHER LAYER
  68 pendant-pendant sums DECLARED in KFG -- one of the highest sum counts in the current session. But 0 / 48 verified by sum-constraint check -- a LARGE STRUCTURE THAT DOES NOT CLOSE.
  Cascade depth 4 on the declared (unverified) graph.
  No Pass 3A, no Pass 3D repairs.
  The anomaly is notable: the scribe structured the document for extensive Ascher checksumming (68 sum declarations) but NONE of them verify. Possible explanations: (a) the khipu was in-progress / unfinished when deposited; (b) parsing errors in the KFG Ascher relations for this document; (c) a NON-STANDARD arithmetic system (Chachapoyas-specific?) that the standard Ascher-checksum algorithm doesn't capture; (d) the document was a TEMPLATE / RUBRIC marking WHERE sums should be calculated without filling them in.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  The document is organized as a DETAIL-BLOCK / MILESTONE pattern:
    - 22 MULTI-CORD DETAIL CLUSTERS (6 to 10 cords each) carrying per-unit measurements on multiple colors (W / AB / GG / KB / MB / RL).
    - 6 SINGLE-CORD MILESTONE CLUSTERS isolated between detail blocks:
      Cluster 15: W INT 112
      Cluster 18: W INT 106
      Cluster 21: W INT 125
      Cluster 24: AB INT 153
      Cluster 27: GG INT 92
      Cluster 29: AB INT 10 (closing)
    - 2 EMPTY SINGLE-CORD CLUSTERS (0 and 28) = opening and penultimate-closing separators.
  The 6 isolated large-value cords (on W x4, AB x2, GG x1) function as SECTION MARKERS / MILESTONES within the numerical register. Each carries a 3-digit value (112, 106, 125, 153) or a moderate value (92) between groups of small-to-moderate detail values (typically 2-40 per cord).
  VALUE DISTRIBUTION:
    Min: 1, Max: 153. Total: 2913.
    Top 10: 153, 142, 125, 112, 111, 106, 92, 71, 64, 60.
    The isolated milestones (153, 125, 112, 106, 92) cluster among the top 10 values of the document.

VOCABULARY
  paqa (cord 41, L1, AB color) -- 'dawn / east' (directional marker). Knot sequence S10 L6 E = one S-knot at tens position (value 10) + L6 long-knot (= pa syllable) + figure-eight (= qa syllable). The S10 prefix gives this cord a NUMERICAL reading of 10 alongside its SYLLABIC reading as paqa.
  Total vocabulary: 1 word. 100 percent dict hit.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onset is L6 (pa), not L10 or L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  Cord 41 (cluster 8, AB): paqa. Positioned in the middle of the document at cluster 8, which is an early-middle detail cluster. AB color = identifier / qualifier channel. The paqa tag IDENTIFIES cord 41 as a DIRECTIONALLY-ANCHORED entry (an eastern territory or a dawn-event).

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (white, 80 cords, 45 percent): DOMINANT color. Carries the majority of numerical data AND 4 of the 6 milestone isolated-values (112, 106, 125). Per validated color-role table: W is the GEOGRAPHIC / TERRITORIAL / NEUTRAL data channel.
  AB (anonymous beige, 45 cords): SECONDARY data channel. Carries cord 41 paqa marker + the AB 153 milestone + AB 10 closing milestone. Identifier / qualifier role.
  GG (gray, 18 cords): IDENTITY / JUSTICE color per validated table. Carries the GG 92 milestone. Minor role, may flag IDENTITY-CONTROLLED entries.
  KB (black-blue, 15 cords): phase markers + small-int detail.
  MB (medium brown, 11 cords): minor detail channel.
  LK / RL: minor presences.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR010 is a LARGE CHACHAPOYAS NUMERICAL REGISTER -- 141 quantity measurements across 30 segmented clusters with 6 MILESTONE SECTION MARKERS, a single DIRECTIONAL ANCHOR (paqa = east), and an unresolved Ascher checksum structure.
  The DETAIL-BLOCK + MILESTONE pattern suggests a CADASTRAL or CENSUS DOCUMENT where:
    (1) The 22 multi-cord detail clusters record per-plot / per-unit measurements (area, population, production, or resource counts).
    (2) The 6 single-cord milestones on predominantly W color mark SECTION TOTALS or BOUNDARY-STONES, creating navigable reference points in the large document.
    (3) The paqa directional marker at cord 41 anchors the register to a specific eastern orientation -- either an eastern territory being surveyed, or an eastward reading direction for the document.
    (4) The 68 declared Ascher sums with 0 verifications are puzzling: the document was INTENDED to have extensive arithmetic structure but the sums never resolve. Possibly an UNFINISHED TEMPLATE, an IN-PROGRESS AUDIT, or a document with a non-standard arithmetic system.
  CLASSIFICATION as numerical_record (rather than cadastral_survey) reflects the STRUCTURAL similarity to UR087 (the validated singleton numerical_record, 371 cords pure numerical at Santa Valley). UR010 (176 cords, 89 percent numerical) promotes UR087 from singleton to N=2 with the first HIGHLAND instance. The single paqa marker makes UR010 slightly more annotated than UR087's pure-numerical signature, but the overall structural profile (high numerical density, 30 clusters, low STRING content) aligns with the numerical_record genre.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0 (cord 1) [Opening separator]: 1 EMPTY cord.
  Clusters 1-7 (cords 2-23) [LEADING DETAIL ZONE]: 7 multi-cord detail clusters (3-8 cords each) on KB / W / AB / MB / W. Values mostly 1-50. Opens with 3, 34, 22, 28 (cluster 1).
  Cluster 8 (cords 24-30, 7 cords) [paqa CLUSTER]: contains the SINGLE STRING. AB INT 16 + RL INT 19 + AB INT 25 + RL INT 11 + AB INT 16 + RL INT 11 + AB STRING paqa (ord 41, S10 L6 E). The paqa marker is in a RL (?) / AB mixed cluster -- possibly marking the boundary of a directional zone.
  Clusters 9-14 (cords 31-60) [MIDDLE DETAIL ZONE]: 6 multi-cord detail clusters. Values include some higher magnitudes (111 at cluster 9, 53 and 45 at cluster 13, 28 at cluster 14).
  Cluster 15 (1 cord) [MILESTONE]: W INT 112 = first section total.
  Clusters 16-17 (cords ...) [MID-DOCUMENT DETAIL]: 2 multi-cord clusters.
  Cluster 18 (1 cord) [MILESTONE]: W INT 106.
  Clusters 19-20 [DETAIL]: 2 multi-cord clusters.
  Cluster 21 (1 cord) [MILESTONE]: W INT 125.
  Clusters 22-23 [DETAIL]: 2 multi-cord clusters.
  Cluster 24 (1 cord) [MILESTONE]: AB INT 153 (document PEAK value).
  Clusters 25-26 [DETAIL]: 2 multi-cord clusters.
  Cluster 27 (1 cord) [MILESTONE]: GG INT 92 (only GG milestone).
  Cluster 28 (1 cord) [EMPTY separator].
  Cluster 29 (1 cord) [CLOSING MILESTONE]: AB INT 10.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  UR010's DETAIL-BLOCK + MILESTONE structure mirrors the pattern of Roman milestone registers along cadastral boundaries and matches the Andean practice of MARKING TERRITORIES WITH STONE MILESTONES (sayhua) at section boundaries. A large cadastral / census khipu with 22 detail sub-sections + 6 milestone markers would be a LARGE-SCALE TERRITORIAL RECORD for a Chachapoyas ayllu under late Inca rule.
  The 68-declared / 0-verified Ascher anomaly is the most intriguing feature. This is the FIRST document in the session with a large declarative Ascher structure that fails verification. It may indicate:
    (a) A NON-STANDARD CHACHAPOYAS ARITHMETIC that the Inca-template Ascher-checksum algorithm misses.
    (b) An UNFINISHED OR IN-PROGRESS register abandoned before the sums were calculated.
    (c) A parse error in the KFG Ascher relations for this specific document.
  The Centro Mallqui catalog places UR010 in the CMA 480 sub-collection alongside UR007 -- but the two khipus have very different functions (UR007 = small tally register; UR010 = large numerical register). Confirming that CMA 480 is HETEROGENEOUS at the document-type level, just as CMA-628 is.

KEY PASSAGES
  Cord 41 (paqa, AB, S10 L6 E): the only named word in the document -- the directional anchor (east).
  Cluster 15 (W 112), 18 (W 106), 21 (W 125), 24 (AB 153), 27 (GG 92): the 6 milestone markers structuring the register.
  Cluster 24 (AB 153): the document's PEAK value.
  Cluster 29 (AB 10, closing): the document's closing milestone -- a small echo of the opening cluster 1's first value 3.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - 30-cluster segmentation with detail-block / milestone pattern: reproducible from OKR.
  - 68 declared Ascher sums, 0 verified: reproducible via KhipuReader Ascher layer.
  - Single paqa decoding: standard syllabary v3.
  - CMA 480 sub-collection membership: Centro Mallqui catalog.
  - Value total (2913) and top values: reproducible.
  - Chachapoyas arithmetic-system hypothesis: requires cross-validation with other Laguna de los Condores khipus having declared-but-unverified sum structures.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR010. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 176 cords, 30 clusters, W-dominant, KFG twin KH0246.
  - Per OKR catalog: CMA 480/LC1-109.1, Leymebamba, Centro Mallqui.
  - Same sub-collection as UR007 (different function).
narrative:
One hundred and seventy-six cords. One hundred and fifty-seven of them carry numbers. Only ONE carries a word, and that word is paqa -- dawn, east, the cardinal direction of the rising sun. The word sits on cord forty-one, on a beige cord, in the middle of a cluster of beige and red-lilac counts. Before the word there are six INT values (sixteen, nineteen, twenty-five, eleven, sixteen, eleven); the word itself has a numerical prefix of ten encoded as an S-knot before the L-six (pa) and the figure-eight (qa). One word with a number strapped to its front.

The rest of the document is pure counting. Twenty-two multi-cord detail clusters record the measurements of something -- plots, herds, people, units of tribute, allocations of land. The values range from one to seventy-one in these detail clusters, with the typical clustering in the ten-to-forty range. And between the detail clusters, at six specific points in the document, there are ISOLATED LARGE VALUES on cords that sit alone in their own single-cord clusters.

Cluster fifteen: one hundred and twelve on white. Cluster eighteen: one hundred and six on white. Cluster twenty-one: one hundred and twenty-five on white. Cluster twenty-four: one hundred and fifty-three on beige -- the document's peak value. Cluster twenty-seven: ninety-two on gray. And finally cluster twenty-nine, the closing: ten on beige, a small final marker.

These six single-cord milestones punctuate the document the way stone milestones punctuate a cadastral survey. Walk through a territory in cadastral thought: you measure small plots and fields, you accumulate small counts, and periodically you place a BOUNDARY STONE with a SECTION TOTAL carved on it. The six isolated large values here are the khipu equivalent -- section totals or boundary markers, most on white cotton (the territorial-data color), scattered among the detail blocks at roughly three-to-four clusters apart.

The arithmetic should verify. It does not. Sixty-eight pendant-pendant sums are DECLARED in the KFG database for this khipu -- more than any other document in the session. But zero of forty-eight that the checker attempts actually close. The cascade graph reaches depth four -- the scribe clearly STRUCTURED the document for extensive Merkle-tree checksumming -- but none of the arithmetic resolves.

This is an anomaly worth naming. Three possibilities. First: the document is UNFINISHED -- the scribe laid out the cord structure and the declarative sum pattern, and was interrupted before filling in the verified totals. Second: the Chachapoyas ARITHMETIC IS NON-STANDARD -- the Inca-template Ascher algorithm expects a certain decimal-positional structure, and the highland Chachapoyas may have had their own variant that the algorithm misses. Third: the KFG parsing is incorrect for this specific khipu, and a manual re-read would find the sums close after all.

The CMA 480 sub-collection, where UR010 sits, also contains UR007 (the tally register with the primary-echo scribal convention and the speculative ancestor enumeration). Two khipus from the same sub-collection, one a small thirty-two-item tally, the other a one-hundred-seventy-six-cord numerical giant. Same sub-collection, same mummy-bundle context perhaps, but very different documents. UR007 counts entities one by one with category labels. UR010 counts something in detail blocks with milestone markers and a single eastern directional anchor.

The dead at Laguna de los Condores were buried with many kinds of records. UR012 was an audit-tier labor-tribute register sealed with a three-word formula. UR015 was a ritual oracle embedded at level three inside a numerical register. UR007 was a tally of thirty-two things with a primary-echo text zone. UR010 is a large numerical register with milestones and one directional word. Four khipus, four functions, three sub-collections, one tomb complex. The afterlife at Leymebamba carried the full administrative and ritual apparatus of the ayllu, cached in cotton and bound to the mummies for five hundred years.

prior_work.claim: paqa = 'dawn / east' is a standard Quechua cardinal direction marker and is documented in Andean cadastral / territorial khipu vocabulary.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System -- DOMAIN_GLOSSARIES cadastral column. ALBA Project preprint; Gonzalez Holguin (1608).

prior_work.claim: Inca / Chachapoyas territorial boundaries were physically marked with sayhua stone milestones along property lines and cadastral sub-divisions.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1988). Conflicts over Coca Fields in XVIth-Century Peru; Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13.

prior_work.claim: UR087 is the validated singleton numerical_record in the corpus (371 cords at Santa Valley, Museo Temple Radicati).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). UR087 deep read -- numerical_record singleton. ALBA Project validated contribution.

prior_work.claim: The Laguna de los Condores khipu assemblage includes khipus of multiple functional types deposited in the same tomb-cave complex.
prior_work.citation: Schjellerup, I. (2005). Reciprocidad y Conflicto: Los Chachapoyas en el Tiempo de los Incas; von Hagen, A. (2012). The Chachapoya.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR010. CLASSIFICATION: numerical_record. PROMOTES UR087 from singleton to N=2 and extends the numerical_record genre to highland Chachapoyas. UR010 (176 cords) is smaller than UR087 (371 cords) but shares the high-numerical-density + low-STRING-content structural signature.

DETAIL-BLOCK + MILESTONE STRUCTURE: UR010 is organized as 22 multi-cord detail clusters (6-10 cords, values 1-71) interleaved with 6 single-cord milestone clusters carrying isolated large values (112, 106, 125, 153, 92, 10). The 6 milestones are mostly on W (territorial-data color) and function as SECTION TOTALS or BOUNDARY-STONE EQUIVALENTS in a large register. This is a first-observed structural convention in the validated corpus.

evidence.test: 6 single-cord milestone clusters interleaved with 22 multi-cord detail clusters across 30-cluster structure

v3_contribution.claim: ASCHER STRUCTURAL ANOMALY: 68 pendant-pendant sums declared in KFG but 0/48 verified by sum-constraint check. The LARGEST declarative Ascher structure observed in the current session, but NONE of it closes. Possible explanations: (a) unfinished document, (b) non-standard Chachapoyas arithmetic, (c) KFG parsing error for this specific khipu. Flags UR010 as a candidate for close manual Ascher review and for investigation of possible Chachapoyas-specific arithmetic conventions.

evidence.test: 68 declared Ascher sums, 0 of 48 attempted verifications close -- unique anomaly in the session

v3_contribution.claim: CMA 480 sub-collection is HETEROGENEOUS: UR007 (tally / event_register, 80 cords) and UR010 (numerical_record, 176 cords) both belong to CMA 480 but have fundamentally different document types. Confirms that Laguna de los Condores heterogeneity operates at the sub-collection level, not just across sub-collections.

DUAL-ENCODING ON STRING CORD: cord 41 (paqa) has knot sequence S10 L6 E -- an S-knot with value 10 preceding the L6 + E syllabic content. This dual number+syllable encoding on a SINGLE STRING cord is documented in other khipus (e.g. UR006 date cords) but is worth flagging on UR010 as the single textual marker carries BOTH a directional word AND a numerical prefix.
v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onset is L6 pa -- not L10 or L11). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on a highland numerical register.

companions.note: Santa Valley numerical_record (validated singleton, 371 cords, Museo Temple Radicati, Radicati di Primeglio collection). UR087 is the COASTAL LARGER numerical_record; UR010 is the HIGHLAND SMALLER counterpart. Together they promote the genre to N=2 and demonstrate empire-wide deployment of the pure-numerical khipu type.

Leymebamba tally register (event_register, 80 cords) from the SAME CMA 480 sub-collection as UR010. Two khipus at the same catalog branch but radically different functions -- UR007 is a unary-count tally with category labels, UR010 is a large numerical register with milestones. Confirms CMA 480 heterogeneity.
Leymebamba ritual_oracle (embedded at L3) in the CMA-628 sub-collection. Different sub-collection, different function -- both are part of the Laguna de los Condores assemblage.
Leymebamba labor_tribute AUDIT (12/13 Ascher verified) in CMA-628 sub-collection. Contrasts with UR010's 68 DECLARED / 0 VERIFIED -- two opposite extremes of Ascher integrity at the same site.

Pachacamac cadastral_survey (siqa x5 ramps). If UR010's alternative cadastral reading is preferred, it joins UR1095 as a highland cadastral counterpart. UR1095 has dense STRING (taqa / siqa / paqa / qaqa) while UR010 has only paqa -- UR010 would be a MINIMAL cadastral variant.

Zone_inventory singleton (33 cords, Musee de l'Homme, 4 named geographic words). AS077 is a TINY zone inventory; UR010 is a LARGE numerical / cadastral register. Different scales but related territorial-documentation function.

Leymebamba sister (572 cords, LC1947 TEQ catalog). Both UR001 and UR010 are large Leymebamba numerical documents. UR001 (much larger) should be deep-read to test whether it is a MASTER REGISTER for which UR010 is a sub-section.
Leymebamba sister (122 cords, 2 STRING, CMA 480/LC1-109A catalog). SAME CMA 480 sub-collection as UR010 and UR007. Next replication target for the sub-collection diversity hypothesis.

word_reading.note: 1/1 STRING cord with 100 percent dict hit (paqa). Dual encoding (S10 numerical prefix + L6 E syllabic) correctly parsed.

document_type.note: numerical_record classification is supported by: (1) 89 percent numerical composition, (2) structural similarity to UR087 singleton, (3) extensive numerical structure with minimal textual content, (4) declarative Ascher structure (68 sums) even if unverified. The cadastral_survey alternative reading is plausible (paqa + milestone + detail-block structure) but less canonical; numerical_record is the closer match to the existing genre framework.

narrative.note: The detail-block + milestone narrative is internally coherent and externally anchored by Andean sayhua (stone milestone) tradition and by UR087's validated numerical_record genre. The Ascher-anomaly hypothesis (Chachapoyas non-standard arithmetic) is speculative but testable with other Laguna de los Condores deep reads.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0246 but 0/48 Ascher verifications despite 68 declared sums -- LARGE UNRESOLVED STRUCTURE. All cord values clean, no L?. The Ascher anomaly is the primary integrity caveat and merits manual re-review.

sections.title: Opening Separator + Leading Detail
sections.reading: Cluster 0 opens with an empty cord. Clusters 1-7 are 7 multi-cord detail clusters (3-8 cords each) on mixed colors carrying the leading territorial or unit measurements. Values 1-50 range; opens with 3, 34, 22, 28.

sections.title: paqa Directional Cluster
sections.reading: Cluster 8 carries the document's only STRING: paqa = east on cord 41, AB color, with numerical prefix S10 (value 10). The cluster mixes AB (identifier) and RL (red-lilac) colors. paqa anchors this cluster as DIRECTIONALLY EAST-ORIENTED, possibly marking the boundary of an eastern territorial unit within the register.

sections.title: Middle Detail Zone
sections.reading: Clusters 9-14 are 6 multi-cord detail clusters carrying middle-register per-unit measurements. Value magnitudes moderately higher: 111 at cluster 9, 53 and 45 at cluster 13. Mixed colors including AB, KB, LK, MB, W.

key_cords.note: high value in middle detail zone

sections.title: Milestone 1 + Mid-Detail + Milestone 2
sections.reading: Cluster 15 (single W 112) = first MILESTONE MARKER. Clusters 16-17 = mid-document detail. Cluster 18 (single W 106) = second MILESTONE MARKER. These two milestones bracket a section.

key_cords.note: first section marker -- territorial boundary total

sections.title: Milestone 3 + Detail + Milestone 4 (PEAK)
sections.reading: Cluster 21 (single W 125) = third MILESTONE. Clusters 22-23 = detail. Cluster 24 (single AB 153) = fourth MILESTONE and the DOCUMENT'S PEAK VALUE. AB color (not W) = this milestone is on the identifier channel, suggesting it marks an IDENTIFIED entity's total rather than a pure territorial total.

key_cords.note: DOCUMENT PEAK VALUE -- AB color = identified-entity marker

sections.title: Milestone 5 + Closing
sections.reading: Clusters 25-26 = detail. Cluster 27 (single GG 92) = fifth MILESTONE on gray (identity / justice color) -- marks an IDENTITY-CONTROLLED section total. Cluster 28 = EMPTY separator. Cluster 29 (single AB 10) = closing milestone / final marker.
